categories.title: ElizaOS Community Discussion: Transition, Governance, and Developer Activity

content.text: A new episode of the Cron Job series titled 'The 4/20 Miracle' (S1E12) was posted on elizaos.news. The episode covers a pivotal week in which a leaked shutdown message became official, the community split between those mourning the project and those continuing to build, scammers became highly active, a DAO conversion was proposed, the CI/CD pipeline experienced issues, a prediction markets plugin was added, and development continued at a strong pace despite the turmoil.

Community member shawmakesmagic posted on X alleging that Baoskee committed financial crimes, market manipulation, and made false promises, claiming these actions led to the token migration and prevented ai16z/elizaOS from exceeding 100 million in value. The post indicated willingness to serve as a chief witness for legal action. Separately, a community member noted that the token has dropped more than others during the downturn and has not recovered during the recent market rally.

A community member raised the question of whether Eliza Labs had fully dissolved, and called for a structured, transparent forum where developers and community members could organize new leadership, address business functions such as social media and website management, and build a productive ecosystem. Odilitime clarified that the foundation is still funded and responsible for the token. In response to interest from community members in contributing, Odilitime offered invitations to a steering group focused on organizing marketing efforts and potentially governance. Multiple members expressed interest in joining.

In the developer channel, a community member asked about alternatives to USDC for agents operating cross-border or in contexts where USDC freezing is a concern. Another developer mentioned having built a payment solution specifically for agent machine-to-machine payments featuring spend-capped JWTs and a kill switch, and expressed interest in shipping an integration if Eliza plugins ever need a payment rail. Additionally, a full stack AI and ML engineer introduced themselves, highlighting experience with autonomous and multi-agent systems, RAG pipelines, voice AI, workflow automation, computer vision, and a broad technology stack including React, Node.js, Python, and various AI and cloud infrastructure tools.

Development on April 23, 2026 centered on framework stability, dependency maintenance, and platform expansion across the eliza and cloud repositories. A major focus was reducing prompt bloat in the action-planner and reflection pipeline to improve context window efficiency. The team performed comprehensive dependency updates including TypeScript v6, gymnasium v1.3.0, commander v14, uuid v14, and vitest v4, along with various Cargo, npm, and yarn group updates to ensure overall stability. On the UI and platform side, the settings interface received polish including dropdown sizing fixes, model picker updates, and header drag-strip corrections. Default role wiring for the Android mobile application was also finalized. Work in progress includes support for multi-calendar Google feeds in the eliza repository, calendar management features in the cloud repository, and the addition of a new flipcoin plugin to the elizaos plugin registry. Eleven additional dependency update pull requests are also currently open. A new issue has been raised requiring attention around agent interoperability, specifically the implementation of decentralized private state verification for agents using local zero-knowledge proofs.
